title: melaleuca aglaia
intro: Melaleuca aglaia, also known as the Australian ironwood or white beech, is a flowering plant in the family Melaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Melaleuca aglaia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
